meaning: vi.毁灭,死亡,腐烂,枯萎vt.毁坏,使麻木
examples: Most of the butterflies perish in the first frosts of autumn.
We are genetically programmed to disintegrate and perish, even under ideal conditions.
If Italy does perish, the euro dies too.
Most perish almost instantly in the relentless battle for scarce shelf-space in Japan's small shops.
Perish premature pasturing of pilots Commercial aviation is experiencing a growing short-age of pilots.
